text:
Market today: Sentiment is broadly constructive - small-caps just had their best H1 since 1991, broad indices holding well, and AI/healthcare crossover news (Anthropic drug discovery) keeps the tech/chip narrative alive. No major macro shock in today's headlines; the real driver for your names is still AI spend momentum and semiconductor demand.

Your holdings: All seven positions show GREEN and carry HOLD/NEW status - nothing is screaming for attention. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF - margin on top of that is double leverage, so a bad tape day hits you hard and fast; keep that stop ($200) honest. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF - tiny, moves violently, margin amplifies any drop; default stop at $21.38, watch it closely.

Watch: (1) Whether AI healthcare news lifts chip names broadly - NVDA/ALAB are on the scanner. (2) Oil's big quarterly drop - watch if that rotates money INTO tech or just signals macro softness.

Bottom line: Your book is fine today, but leveraged positions SOXL and SPAL on margin are your real risk - protect those stops religiously.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your hard line - on margin this 3x ETF can erase weeks of gains in one session; no reason to widen it.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, very volatile - keep stop at $21.38 tight; consider sizing down if margin exposure feels stretched.

No new buys today - scanner shows ALAB (9/12, entry $481.96-$482.54, stop $479.73) as cleanest setup, but price is in daily premium (chasing territory) and there's no fresh catalyst today to justify adding more tech/chip risk on a fully green, margined book.
